text: Wire the end-to-end readâ†’actâ†’reflect loop through a wrapped Claude session
description: Integrate existing pieces into one closed loop: read top aim (get_prioritized_aims) â†’ act with effector tools (aim A) â†’ write reflection back (addReflection). Runs inside a real wrapped Claude session on a schedule, no streamText middle layer. Depends on A; feeds B.

reflections.context: Highest-priority buildable in-phase aim per get_prioritized_aims. Verified before building.
reflections.outcome: Confirmed the readâ†’actâ†’reflect closed loop is already implemented by the watchdog state machine (cited the exact prompt sites). Marked partially; the only gap is real-world effector actions (6119682f, human-dependent). Did not rebuild.
reflections.effectiveness: Evidence-based check via grep prevented rebuilding the very loop executing the check.
reflections.lesson: Repeated finding tonight: the autonomy-loop subtree (2b76f62f cluster) is largely BUILT; nearly every 'buildable' open aim there is already realized by the watchdog. The genuine frontier is human-gated (effector credentials) or design-bearing (cooperation-engine UX), not more loop code.

status.comment: Core closed loop is IMPLEMENTED by the watchdog (the wrapped Claude session this very loop runs in): READ via executeStartWork/ideate prompts ('Check Aimparency MCP for open aims' â†’ worker calls get_prioritized_aims); ACT in WORKING; REFLECT via executeWrapUp ('update aim status and comment and reflection' â†’ addReflection), running autonomously through the EXPLORINGâ†’WORKINGâ†’WRAPPING_UP state machine. Remaining gap = the 'act with EFFECTOR tools' half: real-world actions depend on 6119682f (Composio/native connectors), which is human-dependent on credentials. 'On a schedule' is effectively satisfied by the continuous supervisor loop. Do not rebuild the loop.
